titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Harley 111.B14

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=624 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:22:47.539006+00 | translation moa=625 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:22:49.218016+00

div.p:
Ricardus dei gratia ecclesie sancti Andree humilis minister  vniuersis sancte matris ecclesie filiis totius diocesis sue salutem .
Sciant tam posteri quam presentes nos inplenario capitulo nostro . consilio et assensu cleri nostri dedisse . et presentis scripti
munimine confirmasse ecclesie sancte crucis et canonicis ibidem deo seruientibus . Ecclesiam de egglesbrec . que uaria
capella dicitur . et totam terram quam nos ibi habuimus . uel aliquis antecessorum nostrorum . cum omnibus ecclesie et terre prenominatis
iuste pertinentibus  pro animabus regum . DauiD . Malcolmi . et antecessorum nostrorum . Rodberti . Aernaldi . et auunculi nostri
Alwini . et cunctorum fidelium . in perpetuam elemosinam singulis annis reddendo de terra supradicta unam petram cere capelle nostre et successorum nostrorum ad pentecosten . Quare uolumus et precipimus ut predictam ecclesiam cum terra
prenominata liberam . et quietam . ab omni exactione habeant et possideant . saluis episcopalibus de ecclesia .
et redditu prenominato de terra . His testibus . Galfrido abbate de dunfermelin . Iohanne abbate de Calcho .
Osberto abbate de Iedewrde . Andrea archidiacono . Magistro osberto de merlei . Waltero priore de calcho .
Aiulfo decano . Rodberto fratre episcopi . Iohanne nepote Rodberti episcopi . Rodberto seulfi filio de pert . Magistro herberto .
Alexandro capellano . Magistro Abraham . Henrico capellano archidiaconi . Hec donacio facta est inplenario capitulo
apud berewic celebrato . anno ab incarnatione domini millesimo . centesimo . sexagesimo sexto .

Richard, by the grace of God humble servant of the church of St Andrews, to all sons of Holy Mother Church of the whole of his diocese greeting. Let those to come as well as those present know us to have given by the advice and agreement of our clergy in full chapter, and by the corroboration of the present writing to have made firm to the church of the Holy Cross and to the canons serving God in that place the Church of Falkirk, which is called Varia Capella, and the whole land which we ourselves had there, or any of our ancestors, with everything belonging by right to the aforenamed church and land, for the sake of the souls of the kings David, Malcolm; and of our predecessors, Robert, Arnold; and of our uncle, Alwin; and of each of the faithful; in everlasting alms, by rendering each year in respect of the above-written land one stone of wax to the our chapel and our successors', at Pentecost. Wherefore we will and enjoin that they have and possess the aforesaid church with the aforenamed land free and quit from every exaction, saving episcopal dues in respect of the church, and the afornamed rent in respect of the land. With these witnesses: Geoffrey, abbot of Dunfermline; John, abbot of Kelso; Osbert, abbot of Jedburgh; Andrew the archdeacon; Master Osbert de Merlay; Walter, prior of Kelso; Aiulf the dean; Robert, the bishop's brother; John, Bishop Robert's nephew; Robert of Perth, son of Saewulf; Master Herbert; Alexander the chaplain; Master Abraham; Henry, the archdeacon's chaplain. This donation was made in the full chapter celebrated at Berwick, in 1166th year since the Incarnation.
